verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein with an extracellular Ig-like domain. It stably reaches the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane via its own TM anchor, but only after the acrosomal reaction—a regulated, non-baseline cell state. The protein is expressed exclusively in germline (testis/sperm) and is undetectable as a surface protein on fresh spermatozoa. Surface accessibility is restricted to post-acrosomal-reaction spermatozoa during the fertilization competency window. Both the germline-lineage restriction and activation-state requirement place this in 'contextual' rather than 'yes'.
